narrative.headline: 2018 GMC Terrain: brakes and engine lead owner complaints
narrative.summary: Owners filed 212 complaints with NHTSA. Brakes are the single biggest concern at 35% of reports, tied to 2 crashes and 1 injury. Engine trouble comes next at 24%, then power train at 19% and electrical issues at 17%. Air bags are the smallest group at 7% but carry the most harm, with 7 crashes and 9 injuries. One fire was reported in the engine reports and one in the electrical reports. No deaths were reported.

issues.title: Service Brakes
issues.blurb: The top complaint area: 75 reports (35%). Tied to 2 crashes and 1 injury, so test the brakes carefully before buying.

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: Second most reported: 50 reports (24%). 1 involved a fire, so watch for engine warning lights or a burning smell.

issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: 41 reports (19%), tied to 2 crashes and 2 injuries. Check how the car drives and shifts on a test drive.

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: 35 reports (17%), with 1 crash and 1 fire. Confirm the warning systems and electronics all work.

issues.title: Air Bags
issues.blurb: Smallest group but the most serious: 14 reports (7%), tied to 7 crashes and 9 injuries. Worth a close look despite the lower count.
